title: Haematoxylin-Eosin stain for cryosections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4qqli","text":"This protocol has been successfully used to stain our samples of colonial tunicates sectioned at 12 µm. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1qarc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"752b9","text":"Adapted from Anna Jazwinska and Marguerite Kaczorowski.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"be7of","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cufpu","text":"Note that the solutions need to be filtered after dilution, which takes typically 30 min.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":82,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ao9r4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ar31d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"f4rje","text":"Note that Eosin solutions have to be discarded in the Halogenated waste.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":54,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8u8nt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bhc97","text":"Use Histo-Clear II which is a safer alternative to Xylol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"aku4i","text":"Ammonium iron(III) sulfate [Iron alum] (CAS 7783-83-7)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3l8s7","text":"Sulfuric acid (CAS 7664-93-9)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3g4mf","text":"Hematoxylin (CAS 517-28-2)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"22v92","text":"Eosin Y (CAS 17372-87-1) or Erythrosine B (CAS 16423-68-0; for a redder coloring).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"frmr","text":"Glacial acetic acid","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1e35b","text":"Ethanol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"biges","text":"HCl","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71oli","text":"H2O","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9eseh","text":"Histo-Clear II from National Diagnostics (HS-202) or Xylol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"56an0","text":"Omnimount from National Diagnostics (HS-110).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8uqij","text":"Coverslips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ju97","text":"Cellulose fliters","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5bmi8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1c0as","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mix: - 1 g of Ammonium iron(III) sulfate - 50 mL of dH 2 O - 0.8 g of sulfuric acid
Mix separately: - 0.5 g of hematoxylin dissolved in 50 mL of EtOH (96%)
Pour the water-based mix into the EtOH one, filter if necessary.
Mix: - 250 µL of HCl ( 37 % (v/v) ) - 100 mL of EtOH ( 70 % (v/v) ) Solution can be stored and used to differentiate ~30 slides.
Mix: - 0.1 g of Eosin Y - 100 mL of dH 2 O - 0.5 mL of glacial acetic acid Filter if necessary.
Use Histo-Clear II for the final washes.
Thaw for 00:20:00 or until no more signs of condensation.
Rehydrate the cryosection in tap water 00:05:00
Stain in Haematoxylin 00:12:00 Haematoxylin solution can be reused many times and just needs to be filtered once in a while.
Differentiate each slide by shaking it vigorously in HCl-EtOH 00:00:20
Place in a coplin jar with tap water, place the jar in the sink and let tap water run over it for 00:10:00
Rinse in EtOH 80 % (v/v) (slides could be checked for their Eosin staining).
Start the wash “baths”: EtOH (80%, 100%, 100 %) & HC (I, II, III) 00:03:00 each.
Mount under a coverslip using Omnimount.
creator: Marta Wawrzyniak
